root.description: Absolute path of the scanned root at generation time.

commitSha.description: Commit SHA of the scanned tree; null when not a git repo.

dirty.description: True when the working tree had uncommitted changes at scan time.

GraphMeta.description: Scan provenance — which code this graph describes.

id.description: Stable id, unique within a graph. See nodeId()/instanceId().

flags.description: Degradation markers, e.g. "incomplete", "depth-limited", "unresolved-prop-handler", "external-definition". Absent when clean.

exportName.description: Named or default export, if exported.

props.description: Prop names destructured or accessed from the props object.

renderedText.description: Static text visible in the rendered output — the primary signal for matching a screenshot to a component. i18n keys are expanded to one entry per locale, so a French screenshot matches the same component.

rendersComponents.description: Names of components this component renders in its JSX (deduplicated).

ComponentNode.description: A React component definition — the code, not a usage.

file.description: Path relative to the scan root, POSIX separators.

SourceLocation.description: Where a node lives in the codebase.

source.description: Where the text comes from: JSX children, a string attribute (placeholder/label/title/alt/aria-label), or an i18n key resolved against locale files.

key.description: i18n entries only: the translation key, e.g. "team.title".

locale.description: i18n entries only: which locale this text belongs to.

branch.description: Condition source text when the text renders only in a branch.

template.description: True when the text came from a template literal with runtime parts — unknown segments appear as `*` ("* items in cart") and match as wildcards.

RenderedText.description: One piece of text a component can render, with its provenance.

id.description: Stable id, unique within a graph. See nodeId()/instanceId().

flags.description: Degradation markers, e.g. "incomplete", "depth-limited", "unresolved-prop-handler", "external-definition". Absent when clean.

definitionId.description: Id of the ComponentNode this instantiates.

parentInstanceId.description: Enclosing instance in the render tree. Null until the cross-file instance tree is built (Phase 2.1); the enclosing *definition* is available via the incoming `renders` edge meanwhile.

staticProps.description: Props with statically-known string values at this call site.

InstanceNode.description:
A component as rendered at one specific call site.

The same <DataTable> definition rendered on the Users page and the Invoices page yields two instances — and per-instance data attribution (Phase 2.2) is what lets each report a different API.

id.description: Stable id, unique within a graph. See nodeId()/instanceId().

flags.description: Degradation markers, e.g. "incomplete", "depth-limited", "unresolved-prop-handler", "external-definition". Absent when clean.

HookNode.description: A custom hook — often the bridge between a component and its data.

id.description: Stable id, unique within a graph. See nodeId()/instanceId().

flags.description: Degradation markers, e.g. "incomplete", "depth-limited", "unresolved-prop-handler", "external-definition". Absent when clean.

endpoint.description: Canonical endpoint pattern: constants folded, template placeholders normalized to :param form — e.g. "/api/users/:id". This is the value attribution and cross-graph joins match on.

raw.description: The endpoint expression exactly as written in source.

queryKey.description: react-query/SWR cache key as written in source (e.g. `["users"]`) — the identity used for cache-sharing analysis (C7) alongside the endpoint.

DataSourceNode.description: An external data origin: an HTTP endpoint, GraphQL operation, or socket.

EndpointResolution.description: How much of an endpoint was statically resolvable.

id.description: Stable id, unique within a graph. See nodeId()/instanceId().

flags.description: Degradation markers, e.g. "incomplete", "depth-limited", "unresolved-prop-handler", "external-definition". Absent when clean.

StateNode.description: Local or global state a component reads.

id.description: Stable id, unique within a graph. See nodeId()/instanceId().

flags.description: Degradation markers, e.g. "incomplete", "depth-limited", "unresolved-prop-handler", "external-definition". Absent when clean.

handler.description: Name of the handler function, if resolvable.

EventNode.description: A user or system event a component responds to.

via.description: Prop name for provides-data edges; handler name for triggers.

expression.description: Source text of the condition, e.g. `isEnabled("new-billing")`.

EdgeCondition.description: A statically-detected condition guarding an edge (feature flag, role, branch).
